title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakast accompanies Mrs. Rivallion to identify a body. Upon viewing the deceased on a gurney, she confirms the man is her husband, Harry, noting that although he has aged significantly, his identity is certain. 

Back at the police station, Mrs. Rivallion reveals that Harry used the name Castleton, which she suspects was not his real name. She describes him as an elegant, upper-class man who used his respectable appearance to deceive women. She explains that Harry frequently engaged in patterns of behavior where he would propose investments to women, often after getting them emotionally involved or engaged. Mrs. Rivallion notes that she refused to invest her money in him, both due to a lack of trust and a desire to maintain financial independence.

Mrs. Rivallion recounts that their marriage ended fifteen years ago after Harry claimed he had gotten a schoolteacher into trouble and needed to leave immediately. At the time, she gave him ten pounds and has had no contact with him since. She also reveals that her own name, Melina Rivallion, is not her real name; her true name is Flossi Gapp. She currently works odd jobs, including occasional acting.

When Hakast presents a business card found in the deceased's pocket for an insurance agent, Mrs. Rivallion acknowledges the profession but suggests Harry likely used many different names. Regarding the motive for Harry's presence in the victim's neighborhood, she dismisses the idea of fraudulent insurance scams, suggesting instead that he was likely pursuing women or perhaps engaging in light blackmail by exploiting women's desire to keep their pasts secret.
